lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Welcome to the museum of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you any interesting news?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Yes, we found a rather interesting island to the North of Morytania. We believe that it may be of archaeological significance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh? That sounds interesting.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Indeed. We are making the final preparations for our voyage, in fact, you may be just the person we are looking for... Assuming you've got the necessary qualifications, that is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would I qualify then?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Although you do have the necessary qualifications, I'm not sure you're ready. Come back another time, and we'll discuss further.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Yes indeed. You've helped us a great deal around the Museum and you have the necessary qualifications from the Earth Sciences exams.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: I have received word from the digsite that the canal has been constructed, and the barge is awaiting some final touches.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: The site foreman requested the aid of a qualified and experienced adventurer, and I believe you would be a perfect fit for the role.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sign me up!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: That's great to hear. You should speak to the foreman at the digsite canal, I'll make sure he knows to expect you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I'm far too busy right now.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Fair enough, do let me know if you happen to change your mind.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Welcome to the museum of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: I'm afraid there's not much more I can help you with, Dazaator. The foreman at the digsite should be able to answer any of your questions, good luck.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: The expedition to the island North of Morytania is certainly the most interesting thing going on around here at the moment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, the curator said you could use some assistance here?

lines.speaker: Barge foreman
lines.text: Ah yes, it's about time! We are in the final stages of preparation, there are a few minor adjustments I'd like to make to the barge before we get the crew ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I do to help?

lines.speaker: Barge foreman
lines.text: Well, we've learned from our previous attempts that we need a sturdier type of wood to keep the barge afloat.

lines.speaker: Barge foreman
lines.text: However, magic logs had... well... peculiar side effects.

lines.speaker: Barge foreman
lines.text: Luckily, a new high-grade wood was discovered in distant Western lands, it would make the perfect material for the areas of the barge we need to reinforce.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you'd like me to bring you some of this wood?

lines.speaker: Barge foreman
lines.text: It's not quite that simple, I'm afraid. You'll have to talk to the sawmill operator at the Varrock sawmill to the North West, he will let you know what has to be done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll talk to the sawmill operator.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Barge foreman
lines.text: Welcome back, [player name]. Have you retrieved the supplies yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Barge foreman
lines.text: Well we need them to reinforce the barge, the sawmill operator can tell you what you need to do if you're unsure.

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: Do you want me to make some planks for you? Or would you be interested in some other housing supplies?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here on behalf of the museum archaeological team.

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: Ah, I was wondering when they would finally send over a member of the expedition.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do you need me to do?

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: None of the lumber here is appropriate for the task at hand, however, I've arranged a fairly generous deal between myself and the sawmill over in the Hosidius Woodcutting guild.

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: You'll have to deliver the agreement to their sawmill operator, or a representative of the guild if you lack the requirements to enter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds fairly straight forward.

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: That's good to hear. Once the document has been signed, bring it back to me and we'll get those supplies sent over to the foreman.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, bye.

sections.title: Talking to the sawmill operator again

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: Do you want me to make some planks for you? Or would you be interested in some other housing supplies?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here on behalf of the museum archaeological team.

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: Ah yes. You need to get those documents signed by the sawmill operator in the Woodcutting guild in Hosidius. If you lack the requirements to enter, a representative of the guild can sign it on the sawmill's behalf.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be back with those signed documents.

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: Do you want me to make some planks for you? Or would you be interested in some other housing supplies?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here on behalf of the museum archaeological team.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost the trade proposal...

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: No worries, here's another copy.

lines.speaker: Berry
lines.text: Hello there, what business do you have with the Woodcutting guild?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not too sure what I'm supposed to be doing with this document.

lines.speaker: Berry
lines.text: Everything has been signed, so you just need to take it back to the sawmill operator at the Varrock sawmill.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

sections.title: Reclaiming the trade agreement from Berry

lines.speaker: Berry
lines.text: Hello there, what business do you have with the Woodcutting guild?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost the trade agreement...

lines.speaker: Berry
lines.text: No worries, here's another copy.

lines.speaker: Berry
lines.text: Hello there, what business do you have with the Woodcutting guild?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a proposal for your guild's sawmill, it is from the Varrock sawmill.

lines.speaker: Berry
lines.text: Ah yes, our sawmill operator was expecting you. Do you have the proposal with you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have it here.

lines.speaker: Berry
lines.text: Excellent, hand it over and I'll sign it on behalf of the guild if everything looks in order.

lines.speaker: Berry
lines.text: There you go, we'll ship the redwood planks over to Varrock sawmill immediately.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Redwood planks eh? Those would certainly make for an interesting building material...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, bye.

lines.speaker: Berry
lines.text: Welcome to the woodcutting guild, adventurer.

sections.title: Talking to the Hosidius sawmill operator

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: Do you want me to make some planks for you? Or would you be interested in some other housing supplies?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here on behalf of the museum archaeological team.

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The sawmill operator in Varrock said you were in possession of some special planks?

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: Ah yes, it was no easy feat, but I've managed to make some very high quality lumber out of the logs from the redwoods up there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Redwood planks?

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: Precisely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could they be used to build anything else?

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: Well, it certainly makes for an interesting material, I can imagine it being used for all sorts in the future...

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: Anyway, do you have the proposed deal on you? The sooner we set up our agreement the sooner we can ship these materials.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I don't have it on me right now.

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: Well you'll need to let me see the proposal before I can agree to anything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, of course. I'll be back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have it here.

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: Excellent, hand it over and I'll just make sure everything looks in order.

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: There you go. We'll ship the materials over to the Varrock sawmill immediately.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, bye.

sections.title: Talking to Hosidius sawmill operator again

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: Do you want me to make some planks for you? Or would you be interested in some other housing supplies?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here on behalf of the museum archaeological team.

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not too sure what I'm supposed to be doing with this document.

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: Everything has been signed, so you just need to take it back to the sawmill operator at the Varrock sawmill.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

sections.title: Claiming another trade agreement from the operator

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: Do you want me to make some planks for you? Or would you be interested in some other housing supplies?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here on behalf of the museum archaeological team.

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost the trade agreement...

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: No worries, here's another copy.

sections.title: Returning to the Varrock sawmill operator

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: Do you want me to make some planks for you? Or would you be interested in some other housing supplies?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here on behalf of the museum archaeological team.

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: Great, do you have the documents with you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have it with me.

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: Well, I'll need it before I can send over these supplies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, here you go.

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: That all looks to be in order. I'll get those supplies to the foreman right away.

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: We're all done here, the foreman at the digsite should know what you need to do next.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, bye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Barge foreman
lines.text: The supplies arrived earlier, my men have reinforced the barge using those redwood planks. Thanks for your help, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No problem. Is there anything else you need?

lines.speaker: Barge foreman
lines.text: The barge is prepared for the voyage, so I'm just about done here.

lines.speaker: Barge foreman
lines.text: Now that the barge is ready, you should probably talk to the lead navigator on board.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There doesn't seem to be a gangplank... How am I meant to get on board?

lines.speaker: Barge foreman
lines.text: The barge guard will happily help you on board. Just give him a prod!

sections.title: Talking to the barge foreman again

lines.speaker: Barge foreman
lines.text: Now that the barge is ready, you should probably talk to the lead navigator on board.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There doesn't seem to be a gangplank... How am I meant to get on board?

lines.speaker: Barge foreman
lines.text: The barge guard will happily help you on board. Just give him a prod!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Barge guard
lines.text: Hello there, can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I go onto the barge?

lines.speaker: Barge guard
lines.text: Absolutely, the navigators are waiting for you on board!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Hello. You should talk to the lead navigator.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: John
lines.text: Hello there, the lead navigator should be able to answer any questions you've got.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: You should talk to the lead navigator when you're ready to set sail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: Hello, I'm the lead navigator on the expedition, I take it you're here to help us out?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yep, that would be me.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: Good, because we're going to need your help if we're ever going out on this voyage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's wrong?

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: Well, you may be aware of the previous attempts at this expedition?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, what happened?

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: Well, the first barge had some serious design flaws... it didn't float.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: The second barge suffered from 'feature creep'. Senior museum staff stuck their oar in and added unnecessary features: 'go-faster stripes', a portable fossil centrifuge and a walk-on mini-museum.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: Needless to say, it had... buoyancy issues.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: The third barge made it out to sea, but a special sealant was used that attracted sea monsters. They didn't make it very far.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: The fourth also used a special sealant, this one attracted icebergs.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: The fifth barge crashed into the sixth.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: We lost the seventh one.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: The eighth barge is still here, we think. An accident involving a magical explosion and some talking anchovies means that it is now believed to reside 12 seconds out of phase.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: The ninth version was built using magic logs. The barge gained some form of rudimentary sentience. It developed serious depression and ran itself aground near Crandor.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: So, now do you understand why we need your help?

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: So, now do you understand why we need your help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... Not really.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: The voyage is clearly cursed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... right...

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: Well, [player name], whether you believe it or not, know that none of my men will be going on this expedition unless you can ensure our safety from the curse.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How am I supposed to do that?

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: We're not the first crew to face a cursed voyage, perhaps someone who has experienced one before can give you some advice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any idea where I'd be able to find someone like that?

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: I've heard all kinds of stories at the Rusty Anchor Inn in Port Sarim. I'm sure you'll be able to find out something about cursed voyages if you ask around the folks there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll see what I can learn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe later.

sections.title: Talking to the Lead Navigator again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: I've heard all kinds of stories at the Rusty Anchor Inn in Port Sarim. I'm sure you'll be able to find out something about cursed voyages if you ask around the folks there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll see what I can learn.

lines.speaker: Ahab
lines.text: Arrr, matey!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ever made any cursed voyages?

lines.speaker: Ahab
lines.text: Naarrr, can't say I have [laddie/lassie].

lines.speaker: Longbow Ben
lines.text: Arrr, matey!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ever made any cursed voyages?

lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: Arrr, matey!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ever made any cursed voyages?

lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: Aye! I've sailed many a treacherous journey in my time! What it be to ye?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm meant to be setting sail on a voyage of my own, but the crew won't go without some sort of 'protection'. I was hoping someone here would know of a way to avoid falling victim to the so-called curse.

lines.speaker: Longbow Ben
lines.text: There be nothing 'so-called' about curses, [laddie/lassie/young'un]!

lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: Aye, 'tis a matter we take very seriously. Luckily, there are ways ye can avoid a curse.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! What do I need to do?

lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: Well that all depends on the nature of yer travels!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We are heading out on an expedition to an ancient, previously unexplored, island for Varrock museum.

lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: The museum aye? Ye be diggin' up bones and them sorts then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... I guess so.

lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: Then the way I be seein' it, ye be needin' a lucky charm to do with that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A lucky charm to do with digging up bones?

lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: Aye! I hear folk talking of an odd old man up in the quarry near Paterdomus who sounds as if he might deal in those sorts of things.

lines.speaker: Ahab
lines.text: Bahahahaha! This poor [laddie/lassie/young'un] is sailing a cursed voyage and ye be suggestin' lucky charms?!

lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: What ye be gettin' at, Ahab?

lines.speaker: Ahab
lines.text: Listen here, [laddie/lassie/young'un]. There be no way out of a cursed voyage, however, there are ways one can make it through unscathed...

lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: Aye, just like ye and yer leg, Ahab? Ahahaha!

lines.speaker: Ahab
lines.text: Take no notice of him. I just thought it be worth ye knowing all the options is all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps. What would I need to do to make it through the voyage 'unscathed'?

lines.speaker: Ahab
lines.text: Folk be tellin' tales of a special potion... A 'potion of sealegs' they be callin' it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A potion of sealegs?

lines.speaker: Ahab
lines.text: Aye. The crew simply drink the potion before they be settin' sail, and the next thing they know, they be safely at their destination!

lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: Arrr! Magical potions? Be soundin' like nonsense to me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I guess I'll talk to the navigators and offer both up to the crew to see which they'd prefer before I go gathering them up! Thanks!

lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: Be off with ye then, and talk to them navigators! You're ruinin' me rum!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: Welcome back, [player name]. Any luck finding a way for us to make it through this cursed voyage?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I've got two options, actually.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: Interesting, let's hear them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The first is to use a good luck charm, something related to our expedition - a lucky bone from an odd old man in Paterdomus.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The second is a potion of sealegs, the crew would have to drink it at the start of the voyage.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: Well I'm certainly not putting my crew's life in the hands of some lucky charm! I think I'll go with the potion.

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: I think the lucky charm sounds like a good idea...

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: Oh quiet, boy - you shouldn't believe in such nonsense.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's okay, I can get both.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: Fine by me, as long as you get that potion.

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: [Thanks, mister/Thanks, miss/Thanks]!

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: The apothecary in Varrock might be able to help you out with that potion of sealegs, I'd start by paying him a visit.

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: Don't forget the lucky charm from the odd old man up in Paterdomus!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be back once I've got the potion of sealegs and the lucky charm.

sections.title: Talking to Lead Navigator before getting the potion

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: Any luck getting your hands on that potion of sealegs?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: The apothecary in Varrock might be able to help you out, you should pay him a visit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, bye.

sections.title: Talking to Junior Navigator before getting the lucky charm

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: Hey, have you managed to get me that lucky charm?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: The old man up in Paterdomus collects those sort of things, I'd see if he's going anything he's willing to give up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: I am the Apothecary. I brew potions. Do you need anything specific?

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Do you have the ingredients for the potion?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What were they?

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: You need to bring me 2 bottles of vodka and an unfinished marrentill potion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yep, I've got them here.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Excellent. Hand them over and I'll mix the potion.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: I am the Apothecary. I brew potions. Do you need anything specific?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I'm looking for an unusual potion to get my crew through a 'cursed' voyage.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Aha! Don't tell me... You want a potion of sealegs?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, actually. What's so funny?

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: You'd be amazed at the nonsense these pirates believe! I'll let you in on a little secret, there is no magical potion to save you from the sea's wrath!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then what have you been giving those sailors?

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: A simple herbal remedy containing vodka. Lots of vodka.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Look, if you bring me the ingredients, I'll make you the potion. It's not going to save your crew from sea curses, of course, but it'll certainly put their fears to rest!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I don't really have a choice here, I need that potion. What ingredients do you need?

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Two bottles of vodka and an unfinished marrentill potion, I already have everything else I require.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: I am the Apothecary. I brew potions. Do you need anything specific?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost the potion of sealegs...

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Lucky for you I made extra, try to be more careful this time!

sections.title: Talking to Apothecary after getting the potion

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: I am the Apothecary. I brew potions. Do you need anything specific?

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: How goes the expedition?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We haven't gone yet.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Oh! Well, good luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, bye.

sections.title: Talking to the Odd Old Man

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I was told you might be able to help me get a lucky charm?

lines.speaker: Odd Old Man
lines.text: Oh, yes? I suppose I might have something like that laying around...

lines.speaker: Sack
lines.text: Mumblemumblemumble.

lines.speaker: Odd Old Man
lines.text: Why would you be looking for such an item?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm working with the museum.

lines.speaker: Odd Old Man
lines.text: Is that so...

lines.speaker: Sack
lines.text: Mumblemumble. Mumble.

lines.speaker: Odd Old Man
lines.text: Do you take me for a fool? As if someone working for the museum would be looking for something like that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, that's not what I meant...

lines.speaker: Odd Old Man
lines.text: Oh really... so what would it be then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm making a cursed voyage. We're heading to an island on an archaeological expedition.

lines.speaker: Sack
lines.text: Mumblemumble.

lines.speaker: Odd Old Man
lines.text: Interesting, yes, I may have something that could be of use...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's great, what would you like for it?

lines.speaker: Sack
lines.text: Mumblemumble.

lines.speaker: Odd Old Man
lines.text: Absolutely nothing! You can have it free of charge...

lines.speaker: Odd Old Man
lines.text: Just be sure to bring me back any interesting bones you find on that island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err... Sure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm a collector.

lines.speaker: Odd Old Man
lines.text: Is that so...

lines.speaker: Sack
lines.text: Mumblemumble. Mumble.

lines.speaker: Odd Old Man
lines.text: My collection is not up for grabs! Go find your own!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, that's not what I meant...

lines.speaker: Odd Old Man
lines.text: Oh really... so what would it be then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost the lucky charm you gave me...

lines.speaker: Odd Old Man
lines.text: Lucky for you I have another one here, try to be more careful this time!

sections.title: Talking to Odd Old Man after getting the Bone charm

lines.speaker: Odd Old Man
lines.text: Back already?

lines.speaker: Sack
lines.text: Mumblemumble.

lines.speaker: Odd Old Man
lines.text: Did you find any for me on that expedition of yours?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err... Not yet.

lines.speaker: Odd Old Man
lines.text: Well make sure you keep an eye out for anything particularly interesting!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, bye.

sections.title: Giving Junior Navigator the Bone charm

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: Hey, have you managed to get me that lucky charm?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have it here.

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: Thanks, I'm feeling luckier already!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, I'm sure you are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: Thanks for getting me the lucky charm, [player name]. I'm feeling a lot better about the voyage already.

sections.title: Giving Lead Navigator the sealegs potion

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: Any luck getting your hands on that potion of sealegs?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have it here.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: Excellent work, [player name]! I'm sure the crew will be very glad to hear about this.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: Looks like we've got everything we need to set off!

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: We're ready to go when you are, [player name].

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: What did your potion do to the crew?!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, the apothecary figured they can't be afraid if they're unconcious...

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: But then who's going to get us to the island?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aren't you meant to be a navigator?

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: Yes, a JUNIOR navigator! I've never navigated open waters like this!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are we going to do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I've got some sailing experience myself. Hopefully I can get us there without too much trouble.

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: But this barge is highly specialised! It's the only one of its kind and it took years for the lead navigator to learn how to sail it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, this might not be as straightforward as I thought. What are we going to do?

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: Okay... Let me think...

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: You'll need to operate the barge controls, I'll let you know if we're headed in the right direction.

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: You'll have to fight against the winds to keep the bearing North, you can do that by steering the barge.

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: You can increase or decrease our speed by adjusting the sails. You might find keeping the bearing North easier at slower speeds, but it will take longer to reach the island.

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: If we're headed in the wrong direction for too long I'll take us back to the digsite. Let me know if you want me to go back over these instructions at any point.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to do something first.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: Okay, see you soon.

sections.title: If the player fails the voyage

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: We were completely off course, [player name]. Talk to the lead navigator to try again.

sections.title: Talking to Junior Navigator after failing a voyage
Talking to the Lead Navigator to try again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: My head... What happened? I thought we were going to set off on our voyage, [Player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There were some... complications.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: Well, are you ready to set off again? Let me know and I'll get the crew to take another dose of this potion of sealegs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to do something first.

lines.speaker: Lead Navigator
lines.text: Okay, see you soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: You should talk to the lead navigator when you're ready to set sail.

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: Good work, [player name], you got us here in one piece!

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: I suppose you'll want to start exploring the island as soon as possible.

lines.speaker: Junior Navigator
lines.text: Here, take this journal so you can keep track of your discoveries. Base camp is being set up on shore, let me know if you'd like to head there.
